predicted_questions.question_text: Thanks — congrats on a strong quarter. I have a two‑part question. First, you said bookings for the Mediterranean and West Coast Mexico softened for a few weeks and have since been rebounding — can you quantify how much of that high‑yield inventory is backfilled today (e.g., % of previously available cabins) and whether the booking window/lead times and realized yields on those rebound bookings are roughly back to pre‑COVID norms or still meaningfully lower? Second, on commercial efficiency: you highlighted app adoption, >70% pre‑cruise penetration and a younger guest mix plus the new Royal ONE card — can you give a sense of how S&M/CAC is tracking versus 2019 (either $ per booking or % of revenue) and the normalized level you expect as these digital/loyalty initiatives scale?
predicted_questions.rationale: Frames a two‑part, measurable question consistent with the persona's recurring_concerns.core_topics[0] (booking curve / demand timing) and core_topics[2] (marketing / customer acquisition efficiency). It pulls directly on management's remarks about a short‑term moderation and recent rebound in Mediterranean and West Coast bookings, the commentary that digital penetration has more than doubled (app MAUs 5x) and the CFO's notes on >70% pre‑cruise penetration and an uptick in younger/repeat guests.
